title: chenopodium hircinum f. subtrilobum
intro: Chenopodium hircinum f. subtrilobum is a variant of the Chenopodium hircinum plant, which is a species of goosefoot or lamb's quarters, a type of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium hircinum f. subtrilobum include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Chenopodium hircinum f. subtrilobum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
